[remote KEK rotation detected from last StatusResponse (KeyId changed), rotate DEK seed]
run k8s-kms-plugin key rotation with a new active KEK and keep old KEK available to
decrypt previously encrypted data and reencrypt with new KEK
New active Key ID recieved thanks to Status
return new KEK status, e.g. new CKA_ID
generate a new DEK seed (32 random bytes)
at startup and cache it ; indefinite
KMS v2 encrypt new DEK Seed
{Plaintext:<new DEK seed as base64 encoded JWE []byte>, Uid:<string>}
encrypt new DEK seed with new remote KEK
return new encrypted DEK seed & new KEK ID
KeyId: <new remote KEK ID as string>,
Data encrypted with the old KEK is marked stale by k8s.
New active DEK can Encrypt and Decrypt, whereas
New active KEK can Encrypt and Decrypt, whereas old KEK can only Decrypt.
[For all encrypted objects with the old DEK, reencrypt with new DEK key]
